name: Prosper
description_short: Prosper is a peer-to-peer personal loan marketplace offering $2,000-$50,000 loans with fixed rates from 6.99% APR. SEC-registered, BBB A+ accredited since 2012, with WebBank as lending partner.
description_long:
Prosper Marketplace was founded in 2005 as one of the first peer-to-peer lending platforms in the United States. Headquartered at 221 Main Street in San Francisco, the company connects borrowers seeking personal loans ($2,000-$50,000) with individual and institutional investors who fund those loans. WebBank, a Utah-chartered FDIC-insured industrial bank, is the issuing lender for all Prosper loans.

Prosper's personal loans carry fixed APRs from 6.99% to 35.99% with 3-year or 5-year terms. Origination fees range from 1% to 9.99%. The platform uses its own proprietary credit model alongside traditional credit scores to assess borrowers. Rate checks use soft credit pulls that don't affect credit scores. Loans can be used for debt consolidation, home improvement, medical expenses, auto expenses, and other purposes.

Prosper is notable for being SEC-registered — its loan notes are registered securities, making it one of the most heavily regulated personal lending platforms. The company has facilitated over $26 billion in loans since inception and is NMLS-registered (#111473). It holds BBB A+ accreditation (since 2012) and maintains a Google rating of 2.1/5 from 71 reviews (reflecting its San Francisco office, not overall service quality).

Key caveats: Prosper has a complex regulatory history including a 2008 SEC cease-and-desist order for selling unregistered securities, a $10M class action settlement in 2013, a $3M SEC settlement in 2019 for misleading investor return data, and a significant data breach in 2025 affecting up to 17.6 million records (litigation pending). APRs reaching 35.99% and origination fees up to 9.99% place it at the higher end of personal loan costs for subprime borrowers.

Borrowers comparing personal loan lenders should consider the full range of borrowing and credit-building options available. Those with damaged credit may find personal loans for bad credit more accessible, though typically at higher rates. Debt consolidation loans are specifically designed to combine multiple high-interest balances into a single payment with a lower rate. For credit rebuilding alongside borrowing, credit builder loans and secured credit cards offer structured paths to improving scores over time. Consumers dealing with existing negative items should also explore credit repair services to address inaccuracies before applying, as a cleaner credit report often unlocks better loan terms. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: 221 Main St, Floor 3, San Francisco, CA 94105

tiers.name: Personal Loans
features: Soft credit check for rate preview

pros: SEC-registered platform providing regulatory oversight unusual for personal lending
BBB A+ accredited since 2012 with strong business practices rating
Rate checks use soft credit pulls — no impact to credit score
No prepayment penalties on any loan
Over $26 billion in loans facilitated since 2005
Loan terms of 3 or 5 years provide repayment flexibility
cons: Origination fees up to 9.99% are among the highest in personal lending
APRs reach 35.99% for subprime borrowers — comparable to credit card rates
2025 data breach affected up to 17.6 million records (litigation pending)
History of SEC enforcement actions (2008, 2019) raises governance questions
Google rating of 2.1/5 from office location reviews, though not reflective of loan experience
Not available in Iowa, North Dakota, or West Virginia
best_for: Borrowers with good credit seeking competitive fixed-rate debt consolidation loans
Consumers who value SEC-regulated lending platforms with transparent note structures
People needing $2,000-$50,000 for home improvement, medical, or auto expenses
Borrowers comfortable with peer-to-peer lending model and online-only experience
diagnosis: Prosper is one of the original peer-to-peer lending platforms with over $26B in loans facilitated since 2005. Its SEC registration and BBB A+ accreditation provide meaningful regulatory oversight. Best for borrowers with good credit seeking debt consolidation, though origination fees (up to 9.99%) and APRs (up to 35.99%) are high-end. The 2025 data breach affecting 17.6M records is a significant concern — check their security disclosures before applying.
services: Personal loans ($2,000-$50,000) with fixed APR rates

contact.address: 221 Main St, Floor 3, San Francisco, CA 94105
